race.name: Granfondo Bogotá
race.tagline: High-altitude UCI qualifier in Colombia's cycling capital

climate.overview: Mild spring-like climate at 2,640m elevation; cool temperatures (7-19°C / 45-66°F) in December with possible rain and high altitude effects.[2]

terrain.overview: Road course starting flat, featuring high-altitude Andean climbs (2,500-3,000m) around Embalse de Tominó, with major difficulties late including La Calera climb (8.85km at 4.7% avg).[1][2]

logistics.explanation: Major airport access but limited details on accommodations and registration; new event with solid organizer backing.[2]

length.explanation: 128km gran fondo provides solid endurance test without excess.[1][2]

technicality.explanation: Primarily road with climbs; low technical demands for gravel standards.[1][2]

elevation.explanation: 1,276m gain is moderate but amplified by altitude.[1][2]

climate.explanation: Cool and variable; altitude adds challenge but no extremes.[2]

altitude.explanation: Extreme 2,500-3,000m elevation makes this uniquely punishing.[1][2]

adventure.explanation: Andean loops around reservoirs offer scenic high-plateau exploration.[1]

prestige.explanation: UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier elevates status.[2][3]

race_quality.explanation: Inaugural edition delivered tight sprints and pro-level tactics.[1]

experience.explanation: Bogotá's cycling culture and city finish enhance vibe.[1][2]

community.explanation: Strong local participation (90% Colombian in prior events) with international draw.[1]

field_depth.explanation: 1,500+ riders including elites like Quintero and Severino.[1]

value.explanation: Qualifier status adds value; costs unknown.[2]

expenses.explanation: International travel to Colombia moderate; local appeal high.[2]

cultural_impact.explanation: Showcases Colombia's passion via Urán support and capital streets.[1][2]

biased_opinion.overview: Inaugural UCI Granfondo Bogotá punched above its weight with extreme altitude turning a moderate profile into a sufferfest. La Calera climb and Tominó reservoir loop at 3,000m demand acclimation; tight photo-finish sprints prove it's no gimmick. Perfect for altitude junkies chasing qualifiers in cycling-mad Colombia.

final_verdict.one_liner: Elite high-altitude road qualifier with massive suffering potential.
final_verdict.full_verdict: Bogotá's Andes deliver a brutal debut: 128km with 1,276m gain at nosebleed elevations, won in dramatic sprints by Quintero and Severino amid 1,500 riders. UCI prestige meets local fire—must-do for qualifiers.[1][2]

logistics.overview: Starts/finishes in Bogotá; accessible via major hub but new event means watch for updates.[1][2]
logistics.registration_notes: UCI Gran Fondo qualifiers by age; pricing unknown.[2]

history.overview: Founded 2025 as inaugural UCI Gran Fondo World Series event in Bogotá, organized by UCI Gran Fondo Brazil team with Rigoberto Urán support.[1][2]
history.founder: Team behind UCI Gran Fondo Brazil.
notable_moments: Camilo Camacho Quintero wins men's in 3:02:12 photo-finish.[1]
Camila Cortés Severino takes women's sprint victory.[1]
1,500+ riders tackle high-altitude course around Embalse de Tominó.[1]

course_description.character: Flat start builds to late high-altitude climbs in Andean plateau, finishing fast in Bogotá streets.[1][2]

suffering_zones.label: La Calera climb: 8.85km at 4.7% avg, top 13km from finish.[2]
High-altitude sections topping 3,000m around Embalse de Tominó.[1]

course_description.signature_challenge: La Calera climb at extreme elevation.

citations.title: Severino and Quintero take victory at 2025 UCI Granfondo Colombia in Bogota
Granfondo Bogota - UCI Gran Fondo World Series
